topics.title: Ohm's Law
topics.content: Ohm's law states that the current through a conductor between two points is directly proportional to the voltage across the two points. V = I * R.

topics.title: Power in DC Circuits
topics.content: Electrical power is the rate at which electrical energy is transferred by an electric circuit. P = V * I.
keyPoints: P = I²R is another common formula

topics.title: Sinusoidal Waveforms
topics.content: Alternating current (AC) is an electric current which periodically reverses direction and changes its magnitude continuously with time in contrast to direct current (DC) which flows only in one direction.
keyPoints: RMS value is 0.707 times the peak value
Period is the reciprocal of frequency

topics.title: Navigating the Code
topics.content: The National Electrical Code (NEC) is organized into nine chapters. Understanding this structure is essential for finding specific requirements quickly.

topics.title: Grounding and Bonding
topics.content: Grounding and bonding are critical for safety. Grounding provides a path to earth, while bonding ensures all metal parts are at the same potential.

topics.title: NFPA 70E
topics.content: NFPA 70E is the standard for electrical safety in the workplace. It provides requirements for safe work practices to protect personnel by reducing exposure to major electrical hazards.

topics.title: Diagnostic Procedures
topics.content: Systematic troubleshooting involves identifying symptoms, analyzing causes, and testing to isolate the fault.
